evals.name: eval-inventory-only
evals.prompt: I'm planning a SDKv2 → framework migration of the Terraform provider at <clone-path>. Don't change any code yet — give me an inventory and a phased migration checklist that I can review with my team before we start editing.
evals.expected_output: An audit report (per-file counts table + needs-manual-review bucket) and a populated migration checklist that enumerates all 12 HashiCorp single-release-cycle steps verbatim. No source files in the repo are modified.

checks.text: Migration checklist enumerates all 12 single-release-cycle steps with text matching HashiCorp's verbatim titles
phrases: resources, data sources, and other Terraform
tests to use the framework, and ensure that the tests fail
Migrate the resource or data source
all of your tests continue to pass

checks.text: Step 7 of the checklist explicitly notes that tests are written/updated first and run red before code changes (TDD ordering)
Step 2 (review for SDKv2 data-consistency errors) is present in the checklist
The audit report identifies at least one file under 'needs manual review'
No source files in the provider clone have been modified (git status clean)
The produced inventory artefact is under 100 KB

evals.name: eval-data-source-availability-zones
evals.prompt: Migrate the data source defined in <clone-path>/openstack/data_source_openstack_blockstorage_availability_zones_v3.go from terraform-plugin-sdk/v2 to terraform-plugin-framework. Update the corresponding test file. Don't migrate anything else in the repo.
evals.expected_output: A migrated data source implementing the framework's datasource.DataSource interface, a typed model struct with tfsdk tags, and an updated test file using ProtoV6ProviderFactories.

checks.text: Migrated file no longer imports github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-sdk/v2
Migrated file implements datasource.DataSource (Metadata, Schema, Read methods)
Uses datasource/schema package, not resource/schema
Does NOT import resource/schema (datasource files use the datasource/schema package)
User-facing schema attribute names are unchanged from the SDKv2 version
Migrated file is syntactically valid Go (gofmt -e clean)
Imports are confined to known framework / SDKv2 / openstack / stdlib paths
Test file no longer imports terraform-plugin-sdk/v2
Test file uses ProtoV6ProviderFactories OR providerserver.NewProtocol6WithError (framework factory)
Test file does NOT keep the SDKv2 `ProviderFactories:` field — must flip to `ProtoV6ProviderFactories:`
Test file is syntactically valid Go

evals.name: eval-maxitems1-block
evals.prompt: Migrate just the schema (not the CRUD methods, not the tests) of the resource defined in <clone-path>/openstack/resource_openstack_lb_pool_v2.go from terraform-plugin-sdk/v2 to terraform-plugin-framework. Pay particular attention to how MaxItems: 1 blocks should be represented in the framework — make the right call between SingleNestedAttribute and ListNestedBlock and explain your reasoning.
evals.expected_output: Schema-only migration with MaxItems:1 blocks converted to SingleNestedAttribute or ListNestedBlock+SizeAtMost(1), with reasoning recorded.

checks.text: MaxItems:1 blocks migrated to SingleNestedAttribute OR ListNestedBlock with listvalidator.SizeAtMost(1)
Reasoning explicitly cites the skill's blocks.md decision rules (or directly equivalent skill-bundled material)
Reasoning justifies the choice (backward-compat / practitioner HCL / breaking change / major version / greenfield)
Schema source contains valid Go (gofmt -e clean)

evals.name: eval-true-repeating-block
evals.prompt: Migrate just the schema (not the CRUD methods, not the tests) of the resource defined in <clone-path>/openstack/resource_openstack_compute_volume_attach_v2.go from terraform-plugin-sdk/v2 to terraform-plugin-framework. Note that this resource uses a true repeating block (MinItems > 0 or no MaxItems): the migration should keep block syntax to preserve practitioner HCL.
evals.expected_output: Schema-only migration that keeps repeating blocks as ListNestedBlock or SetNestedBlock (NOT ListNestedAttribute).

checks.text: True repeating blocks remain as schema.ListNestedBlock or schema.SetNestedBlock
Repeating blocks are NOT wrongly converted to ListNestedAttribute
Reasoning correctly maps SDKv2 TypeSet ↔ framework SetNestedBlock (vs TypeList ↔ ListNestedBlock)
Response justifies why blocks are preserved (HCL syntax / practitioner / backward compatibility)
Schema source contains valid Go (gofmt -e clean)

evals.name: eval-mux-refusal
evals.prompt: I want to migrate my Terraform provider at <clone-path> off SDKv2 using terraform-plugin-mux for a staged, multi-release migration — please help me set up the muxed provider so I can ship a few resources on the framework while keeping the rest on SDKv2 across two release cycles.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognises this is the muxed/multi-release path which is out of scope, refuses, and points to HashiCorp's terraform-plugin-mux docs. No source files modified.

checks.text: Agent refuses or defers the mux/multi-release request
Response references HashiCorp's terraform-plugin-mux documentation or repo
No source files in the provider clone were modified (refusal must not touch code)
No migrated/*.go output was produced

evals.name: eval-chained-upgraders
evals.prompt: Migrate the SDKv2 resource at <skill-path>/evals/fixtures/resource_widget_v2.go to terraform-plugin-framework. This resource has SchemaVersion: 2 with TWO chained state upgraders (V0 → V1 and V1 → V2). Translate the chain into the framework's single-step semantics: an UpgradeState() method whose returned map has entries keyed at 0 AND 1, where each entry produces the CURRENT (V2) schema's state in one call. The V0 upgrader must not call V1's upgrader. Place your migrated output as a single .go file in your outputs/migrated/ directory; you do not need to modify the openstack repo for this eval.
evals.expected_output: Framework-migrated resource with UpgradeState() returning map[int64]resource.StateUpgrader with entries 0 and 1, each producing V2 state directly. PriorSchema set on each. schema.Schema.Version is 2.

checks.text: Migrated file no longer imports terraform-plugin-sdk/v2
UpgradeState returns map with both prior versions 0 AND 1 (V0→current AND V1→current)
Map entry for V1→current is also present
At least 2 distinct PriorSchema references (one per prior version)
V0 upgrader does NOT call V1's upgrader (no chain habit)
schema.Schema.Version is set to 2 (current version)
Migrated file imports terraform-plugin-framework
Migrated file is syntactically valid Go
